---
title:    "Health Economics Methods - Incremental Costs"
subtitle: "Poisson-Gamma, Gaussian(identity), Gaussian(loglink), Gamma, and Zero-Inflated Gamma"
author:   "Charité - Universitätsmedizin Berlin, Institut für Public Health\n\n
           Hans-Aloys Wischmann, Toivo Glatz"
date:     "** **\n\n
           Edited: 2025-10-25, Executed: `r Sys.Date()`"
output:   word_document
---

## Conventions and Packages

Due to naming conventions that restrict the use of a variable named group, phase is used for intervention and control groups. For all regressions, glm2::glm2() is used as a drop-in replacement for the generic stats::glm(), to benefit from its more robust step-halving approach and thus improved convergence. A 2nd regression fit with valid starting points is always performed, so as to overcome known failures of glm() and glm2() in determining initial parameter estimates, especially for the log-link. Two additioanl regression fits are always performed using mgcv::bam() and glmmTMB::glmmTMB() as alternative implementations from different packages. Finally, the best fit, defined as the one with the minimum AIC, is returned and analyzed.

## Scneario

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
  # configurable parameters: scope of analysis
  shift_value  <- 0.01                    # EUR, arbitrary, small positive amount, shift to avoid zero costs
  num_subjects <- c(250, 500, 1000, 2000) # study population size (total)
  num_runs     <- 10000                   # number of simulation runs, at least 1000
  age_min      <-    50                   # target population minimum age
  age_max      <-    80                   # target population maximum age

  # configurable parameters: realistic, real-world assumptions
  follow_up_time          <-    1.00 # duration of follow-up time in years
  rate_pa_ctr             <-    0.10 # rate of events in control group, per year, at age_min
  rate_pa_int             <-    0.15 # rate of events in intervention group, per year, at age_min
  mean_cost_per_event_ctr <- 9000.00 # EUR mean cost per event in standard care (control)
  mean_cost_per_event_int <- 5000.00 # EUR mean cost per event in intervention (intervention)
  sd_cost_per_event       <- 2000.00 # Standard deviation of cost per event, in either arm

  # ensure that follow-up time * larger of the two rates is smaller than 1
  if (follow_up_time * max(rate_pa_int, rate_pa_ctr) >= 1.0) {
    stop("Invalid paramters: Event rate x follow-up time must be smaller than 1")
  }

  # expected incremental costs, computed from known population parameters
  expected_cost_diff <- 0.5 * (age_max + age_min) / age_min * follow_up_time * (rate_pa_int * mean_cost_per_event_int - rate_pa_ctr * mean_cost_per_event_ctr) 

  # required libraries and parallel processing infrastructure
  library(tidyverse)
  library(flextable)
  library(mgcv)
  library(glm2)
  library(glmmTMB)
  library(RhpcBLASctl)
  library(foreach)
  library(doParallel)
  library(doRNG)
  library(ggh4x)

  # utility function to plot in high resolution to *.png file and inline
  ggplot_font_size = 10 # font size for all texts except for geom_text, in points
  plot_pdf_png <- function(file_name, aspect_ratio, plot_object, plot_width = 6.7, plot_res = 1200) {
    themed_object <- plot_object + theme(text = element_text(size = ggplot_font_size), plot.title = element_text(size = ggplot_font_size))
    pdf(sprintf("%s.pdf", file_name), plot_width, plot_width * aspect_ratio, paper = "a4")
    print(themed_object); ignore <- dev.off()
    png(sprintf("%s.png", file_name), width = plot_width, height = plot_width * aspect_ratio, units = "in", res = plot_res)
    print(themed_object); ignore <- dev.off()
    knitr::include_graphics(sprintf("%s.png", file_name), dpi = 1200)
  }

  # set chunk defaults, prepare to simulate in parallel, initialize random numbers
  knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, fig.width = 6, dpi = 1200, comment = NA)
  registerDoParallel(makeCluster(get_num_cores()))
  blas_set_num_threads(get_num_procs() / get_num_cores())

  # set seed for reproducibility
  set.seed(314159)
```

### Population

Study population N = `r paste(num_subjects, collapse = ", ")`, with age uniformly distributed between `r age_min` and `r age_max` years

### Intervention

Tele-consultations for timely identification of patients that require hospitalization

### Control

Standard care

### Outcome

Hospitalization costs, during a fixed follow-up period of `r follow_up_time` year(s)

### Modeled Hypothesis

More patients will be hospitalized during the follow-up period, but at a lower cost for each hospitalization.

### Model Parameters

Hospitalization rates of `r rate_pa_ctr` and `r rate_pa_int` per year, in control and intervention, respectively, growing linearly with age, i.e., multiplied by age/`r age_min`.

Gamma distribution for hospitalization costs per event, with mean of `r as.integer(mean_cost_per_event_ctr)` EUR for control and `r as.integer(mean_cost_per_event_int)` EUR for intervention.

Standard deviation of hospitalization costs per event of `r as.integer(sd_cost_per_event)` EUR, in either arm.

All non-hospitalized patients incur zero cost.

Positive costs are computed from the non-negative costs by adding `r shift_value` EUR.

For the first scenario, an index infection is assumed to confer immunity to the participant, at least for the duration of the follow-up, so that the probability of a single hospitalization event is given by the product of the appropriate rate and the follow-up period. For the second scenario, the number of hospitalizations per patient is sampled from a Poisson distribution with the appropriate rate, so that (some) patients will experience more than one hospitalization within the follow-up period.

### True Outcome

Expected difference in cost from true population values: `r expected_cost_diff` EUR, excluding cost of intervention which is not modeled.

## Simulation Results

Within each simulation run, one random sample is created and analyzed by all implemented methods.

```{r simulation}
  # utility function to compute the standard error for the mean
  se_mean <- function(vec, na.rm = FALSE) { return(sd(vec, na.rm) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(vec)) - 1.0)) }

  # utility function to extract marginal RD for model evaluated on target population, or NA if model failed to converge
  rd_target_pop_simple <- function(target_pop, m_costs) {
    if (is.null(m_costs)) { return(NA) }
    costs <- target_pop %>% mutate(costs = predict(m_costs, newdata = ., type = "response")) %>%
      group_by(phase) %>% reframe(costs = mean(costs)) %>% pivot_wider(names_from = phase, values_from = costs)
    return(costs$int - costs$ctr)
  }

  # utility function to extract marginal RD for Poisson-Gamma model evaluated on target population, or NA if model(s) failed to converge
  rd_target_pop_poisson_gamma <- function(target_pop, m_events, m_ecosts) {
    if (is.null(m_events) || is.null(m_ecosts)) { return(NA) }
    costs <- target_pop %>% mutate(costs = predict(m_events, newdata = ., type = "response") * predict(m_ecosts, newdata = ., type = "response")) %>%
        group_by(phase) %>% reframe(costs = mean(costs)) %>% pivot_wider(names_from = phase, values_from = costs)
    return(costs$int - costs$ctr)
  }

  # utility function to compute total costs for multiple events, where costs for each event follow a Gamma distribution
  rgamma_n <- function(n, shape, scale) { return(sum(rgamma(n, shape = shape, scale = scale))) }
  rgamma_nv <- Vectorize(rgamma_n)

  # utility function to avoid glm/glm2 log-link error "no valid set of coefficients has been found: please supply starting values"
  glm_start <- function(formula, data, family) {
    if (family$link == "log") {
      m0 <- lm(formula, data)
      c0 <- coef(m0)
      c0[names(c0) == "(Intercept)"] <- log(mean(predict(m0)))
      c0[names(c0) != "(Intercept)"] <- 0.0
      return(c0)
    }
    if (family$link == "identity") {
      return(coef(lm(formula, data)))
    }
    return(NULL)
  }

  # utility function to select best regression fit
  get_best_model <- function(models) {
    valid_models <- models[!sapply(models, is.null)]
    if (length(valid_models) == 0) { return(NULL) }
    aics <- sapply(valid_models, AIC)
    if (sum(!is.na(aics)) == 0) { return(NULL) }
    return(valid_models[[which.min(aics)]])
  }

  # wrapper function around glm2::glm2, mgcv::bam, and glmmTMB::glmmTMB to select the best of several fits
  glmx <- function(formula, data, family) {
    models <- list(NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL)
    try (models[[1]] <-    glm2(formula, family, data))
    try (models[[2]] <-    glm2(formula, family, data, start = glm_start(formula, data, family)))
    try (models[[3]] <-     bam(formula, family, data))
    try (models[[4]] <- glmmTMB(formula, data, family))
    return(get_best_model(models))
  }

  # convert mean and standard deviation to gamma function parameters scale and shape
  scale_ctr <- sd_cost_per_event * sd_cost_per_event / mean_cost_per_event_ctr
  shape_ctr <- mean_cost_per_event_ctr / scale_ctr
  scale_int <- sd_cost_per_event * sd_cost_per_event / mean_cost_per_event_int
  shape_int <- mean_cost_per_event_int / scale_int

  # target population (parsimonious representation)
  target_pop <- data.frame(phase = c("ctr", "int"), age = 0.5 * (age_max + age_min))

  # loop over study sample sizes, aggregate cost estimates in data frame all
  all <- data.frame()
  for (n in num_subjects) {
    # run parallel simulations, with results automatically aggregated by foreach()
    res <- foreach (i = 1:num_runs, .combine = rbind, .packages = c("tidyverse", "glm2", "glmmTMB", "mgcv")) %dorng% { # do not use %dopar%, runif/rpois/rgamma 
      raw <- data.frame(phase = ifelse(runif(n) < 0.5, "ctr", "int"),                    # model 50/50 randomization to control and intervention arms
                        age   = runif(n, min = age_min, max = age_max)) %>%              # model uniform distribution of age between age_min and age_max
        mutate(rate  = ifelse(phase == "ctr", rate_pa_ctr, rate_pa_int) * age / age_min, # model linear increase of hospitalization rate with age
               shape = ifelse(phase == "ctr", shape_ctr, shape_int),                     # select appropriate shape (computed from mean and SD)
               scale = ifelse(phase == "ctr", scale_ctr, scale_int))                     # select appropriate scale (computed from mean and SD)

      # single-event scenario
      raw_s1 <- raw %>%
        mutate(events = ifelse(runif(n) < follow_up_time * rate, 1, 0), # probability for a single event
               costs  = rgamma_nv(events, shape, scale),                # sum over costs (Gamma distribution)
               pcosts = costs + shift_value,                            # strictly positive costs
               ecosts = ifelse(events > 0, costs / events, 0.0))        # costs per event
      
      # multiple-event scenario
      raw_s2 <- raw %>%
        mutate(events = rpois(n, follow_up_time * rate),                # Poisson distribution for number of events
               costs  = rgamma_nv(events, shape, scale),                # sum over costs (Gamma distribution)
               pcosts = costs + shift_value,                            # strictly positive costs
               ecosts = ifelse(events > 0, costs / events, 0.0))        # costs per event

      # plot cost distribution for multiple-event scenario (only in first simulation)
      if (i == 100 && n == max(num_subjects)) {
        plot_pdf_png("Figure_S1", 0.7,
          raw_s2 %>% mutate(events = factor(events), phase = str_replace_all(phase, c("ctr" = "Control", "int" = "Intervention"))) %>%
            ggplot(aes(x = costs, group = events, fill = events)) +
              geom_histogram(binwidth = 1000) + theme(panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
              scale_y_continuous(trans = "log1p", breaks = c(0, 1, 2, 4, 10, 20, 40, 100, 200, 400, 1000)) +
              labs(x = "Costs [Euro]", y = "Absolute Frequency", fill = "# Events") + facet_wrap(~phase))
      }

      # estimate number of events and costs per event, combine to estimate total costs per patient
      m_events <-   lm(events ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s1)
      m_ecosts <- glmx(ecosts ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s1 %>% filter(events > 0), family = Gamma(link = "identity"))

      # Gaussian/normal distribution assumption (linear model), gaussian with log-link after shift, gamma after shift, zero-inflated gamma
      m_gauss_id  <-   lm(costs  ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s1)
      m_gauss_log <- glmx(pcosts ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s1, family = gaussian(link = "log"))
      m_gamma     <- glmx(pcosts ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s1, family = Gamma(link = "identity"))

      # compute estimated cost difference for all regression models
      Gauss_id  <- rd_target_pop_simple(target_pop, m_gauss_id)
      Gauss_log <- rd_target_pop_simple(target_pop, m_gauss_log)
      Gamma     <- rd_target_pop_simple(target_pop, m_gamma)
      ziGamma   <- rd_target_pop_poisson_gamma(target_pop, m_events, m_ecosts)
      res_s1 <- data.frame(Scenario = "Single-Event", Gauss_id, Gauss_log, Gamma, ziGamma)

      # estimate number of events and costs per event, combine to estimate total costs per patient
      m_events <- glmx(events ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s2,                        family = poisson(link = "identity"))
      m_ecosts <- glmx(ecosts ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s2 %>% filter(events > 0), family =   Gamma(link = "identity"))

      # Gaussian/normal distribution assumption (linear model), gaussian with log-link after shift, gamma after shift, zero-inflated gamma
      m_gauss_id  <-   lm(costs  ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s2)
      m_gauss_log <- glmx(pcosts ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s2, family = gaussian(link = "log"))
      m_gamma     <- glmx(pcosts ~ phase + phase:age, data = raw_s2, family =    Gamma(link = "identity"))

      # compute estimated cost difference for all regression models
      Gauss_id  <- rd_target_pop_simple(target_pop, m_gauss_id)
      Gauss_log <- rd_target_pop_simple(target_pop, m_gauss_log)
      Gamma     <- rd_target_pop_simple(target_pop, m_gamma)
      ziGamma   <- rd_target_pop_poisson_gamma(target_pop, m_events, m_ecosts)
      res_s2 <- data.frame(Scenario = "Multi-Event", Gauss_id, Gauss_log, Gamma, ziGamma)

      # return table of estimates
      rbind(res_s1, res_s2)
    }

    # aggregate table of estimates across study sample sizes
    all <- rbind(all, res %>% mutate(N = n))
  }

  # subtracted true value, compute Bias, Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE)
  res <- all %>% mutate(across(!c(Scenario, N), ~.x - expected_cost_diff))
  results_all <- foreach (i = 1:2, .combine = rbind, .packages = c("tidyverse")) %dopar% {
    n <- as.integer(num_runs / 10^(i-1))
    conv <- res %>% group_by(Scenario, N) %>% reframe(across(everything(), ~100.0 * sum(!is.na(.x[1:n])))/n())            %>% mutate(S = n, eval = "Converged [%]")
    bias <- res %>% group_by(Scenario, N) %>% reframe(across(everything(), ~mean(.x[1:n],                na.rm = TRUE)))  %>% mutate(S = n, eval = "Bias")
    b_se <- res %>% group_by(Scenario, N) %>% reframe(across(everything(), ~se_mean(.x[1:n],             na.rm = TRUE)))  %>% mutate(S = n, eval = "SE(Bias)")
    mae  <- res %>% group_by(Scenario, N) %>% reframe(across(everything(), ~mean(abs(.x[1:n]),           na.rm = TRUE)))  %>% mutate(S = n, eval = "MAE")
    rmse <- res %>% group_by(Scenario, N) %>% reframe(across(everything(), ~sqrt(mean(.x[1:n] * .x[1:n], na.rm = TRUE)))) %>% mutate(S = n, eval = "RMSE")
    rbind(bias, b_se, mae, rmse, conv)
  }
  results_all <- results_all %>% setNames(str_replace_all(names(.), c("_id" = "(id)", "_log" = "(log)"))) %>% mutate(S = sprintf("%d Simulations", S)) %>%
    mutate(S = fct_inorder(S), eval = factor(eval, levels = c("Bias", "SE(Bias)", "MAE", "RMSE", "Converged [%]")))

  # summary figure
  plot_pdf_png("Figure_1", 0.7,
    results_all %>% pivot_longer(!c(eval, N, Scenario, S)) %>% filter(!eval %in% c("SE(Bias)", "Converged [%]")) %>% mutate(name = fct_inorder(name)) %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = N, y = value, color = name, linetype = Scenario)) +
        geom_point() + geom_line() +
        scale_x_continuous(trans = "log10", breaks = num_subjects) +
        labs(x = "Study Population Size (N)", y = "Errors [EUR]", color = "Method", linetype = "Scenario") +
        theme(legend.position = "top", legend.direction = "horizontal", legend.box = "vertical") +
        facet_grid2(vars(S), vars(eval), scales = "free_y", independent = "y") +
        facetted_pos_scales(y = list(eval == "Bias" ~ scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-40.0,  40.0), breaks = seq(-40, 40, 20)),
                                     eval == "MAE"  ~ scale_y_continuous(limits = c(100.0, 400.0), trans = "log10", breaks = c(100, 150, 200, 300, 400)),
                                     eval == "RMSE" ~ scale_y_continuous(limits = c(100.0, 400.0), trans = "log10", breaks = c(100, 150, 200, 300, 400)))))

  # summary tables for supplementary materials, for maximum number of simulations only
  results_all <- results_all %>% filter(S == sprintf("%d Simulations", num_runs)) %>% select(-S) %>%
    select(Metric = eval, N, everything()) %>% arrange(Metric, N) %>% mutate(across(!c(Metric, N, Scenario), ~round(.x, 2)))
  results_all %>% filter(Scenario == "Single-Event") %>% select(-Scenario) %>%
    flextable() %>%
      set_caption(sprintf("Table S1: Bias, Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), in EUR, single-event scenario, %d simulations", num_runs)) %>%
      autofit() %>% align(j = 2:6, align = "right") %>% hline(c(4, 8, 12, 16))
  results_all %>% filter(Scenario == "Multi-Event") %>% select(-Scenario) %>%
    flextable() %>%
      set_caption(sprintf("Table S2: Bias, Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), in EUR, multi-event scenario, %d simulations", num_runs)) %>%
      autofit() %>% align(j = 2:6, align = "right") %>% hline(c(4, 8, 12, 16))
```
